jelatyna-backend/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Boot parent version to 3.3.13 to align with Java 21 and modern Spring ecosystem.
jelatyna-backend/pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 1.5.x does not support Java 21; the application will fail to compile or run due to incompatible bytecode and removed APIs.

jelatyna-backend/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property to 21 to target the new JDK.
jelatyna-backend/pom.xml.why_exists: The compiler and runtime must target Java 21; using Java 8 settings with Java 21 libraries or features causes compilation errors or runtime incompatibilities.

jelatyna-backend/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Lombok dependency to a version compatible with Java 21.
jelatyna-backend/pom.xml.why_exists: Older Lombok versions (1.16.x) do not support Java 21 syntax and annotations, leading to compilation failures.

jelatyna-backend/pom.xml.general_idea: Remove explicit version from jackson-datatype-jsr310 to rely on Spring Boot's managed BOM.
jelatyna-backend/pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.x manages Jackson versions; specifying an old version (2.8.6) causes dependency conflicts and potential runtime errors with newer Spring Boot components.

jelatyna-backend/pom.xml.general_idea: Update MySQL connector artifact ID and group ID to the new naming convention required by Java 21/Spring Boot 3.
jelatyna-backend/pom.xml.why_exists: The old artifact 'mysql-connector-java' is deprecated/removed in newer repositories; the new 'mysql-connector-j' is required for compatibility with Java 21 and Spring Boot 3.

jelatyna-backend/src/main/java/com/examp.general_idea: Replace deprecated JPA method getOne with getReferenceById to comply with Spring Data JPA 3.x API changes.
jelatyna-backend/src/main/java/com/examp.why_exists: Method getOne() was removed in Spring Data JPA 3.x (required by Spring Boot 3.x/Java 21 stack), causing a compile error.

jelatyna-backend/src/main/java/com/examp.general_idea: Remove import of the deprecated WebMvcConfigurerAdapter class.
jelatyna-backend/src/main/java/com/examp.why_exists: WebMvcConfigurerAdapter was removed in Spring Framework 6.0 (required by Spring Boot 3.x), causing a compile error if imported or used.

jelatyna-backend/src/main/java/com/examp.general_idea: Replace anonymous inner class extending deprecated WebMvcConfigurerAdapter with direct implementation of WebMvcConfigurer interface.
jelatyna-backend/src/main/java/com/examp.why_exists: WebMvcConfigurerAdapter class no longer exists in Spring Framework 6.0+, so code extending it fails to compile.

jelatyna-backend/src/main/java/com/examp.general_idea: Migrate annotation import from javax.annotation to jakarta.annotation to align with Spring Boot 3 and Jakarta EE 9+ namespace requirements.
jelatyna-backend/src/main/java/com/examp.why_exists: The javax.annotation package is no longer available in the default classpath of Spring Boot 3 applications (which run on Jakarta EE 9+), causing a compile-time error: 'package javax.annotation does not exist'.

jelatyna-backend/src/main/java/com/examp.general_idea: Migrate JPA annotations from javax.persistence to jakarta.persistence to align with Jakarta EE 9+ standards required by Spring Boot 3.
jelatyna-backend/src/main/java/com/examp.why_exists: javax.persistence package is removed in Jakarta EE 9+; code fails to compile with 'package javax.persistence does not exist' errors on JDK 21 with Spring Boot 3.

jelatyna-backend/src/main/java/com/examp.general_idea: Migrate JPA entity annotations from javax.persistence to jakarta.persistence to align with Jakarta EE 9+ standards required by Spring Boot 3.
jelatyna-backend/src/main/java/com/examp.why_exists: javax.persistence package is removed in Jakarta EE 9+; code fails to compile with 'package javax.persistence does not exist' on JDK 21 with Spring Boot 3.

jelatyna-backend/src/test/java/com/examp.general_idea: Migrate JUnit 4 annotations and imports to JUnit 5 (Jupiter) to align with Spring Boot 3.x requirements.
jelatyna-backend/src/test/java/com/examp.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.x requires JUnit 5; JUnit 4 runner and annotations are incompatible or deprecated, leading to test execution failures or compile errors if old imports are retained with new dependencies.
